title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Sam Neill stars in 'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople,' streaming on Prime Video and Plex.
'Obsession,' a horror hit of 2026, is available on Peacock after grossing $400 million worldwide.
'Lucky,' featuring Anya Taylor-Joy, debuts with seven episodes on Apple TV.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin, 25, was found to have violated New Hampshire’s Civil Rights Act for assaulting a transgender woman.
The assault occurred in May 2024 at a Speedway convenience store in Concord, resulting in multiple injuries to the victim.
Lufkin received civil penalties and was prosecuted for second-degree and simple assault, resulting in a suspended sentence.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox's trade deadline plans remain uncertain as the Aug. 3 MLB deadline approaches, according to ESPN's Jeff Passan.
With a recent 10-game winning streak, Boston considers adding players but may also sell if performance declines before the deadline.
ESPN's Buster Olney reports the Red Sox seek a right-handed hitter, potentially taking on a bad contract to fulfill this need.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket, owned by Evan and Maria Marley, is for sale for $12.5 million.
The property includes a 46-seat restaurant, staff housing, wood fire oven, and parking for 20 vehicles.
Evan Marley plans to stay during the transition, as they seek a buyer to continue their 25-year legacy.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: The France national soccer team left Boston, their base since June, after a month-long stay at the Four Seasons Hotel.
France heads to Miami to play England for third place after losing 2-0 to Spain in the World Cup semifinals.
The team practiced at Bentley University in Waltham and rented out the entire hotel during their Boston stay.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon's production company, Artists Equity, is in early development of a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of charges in the 2022 death of her boyfriend, Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe.
Several film projects on Read's case are in progress, including a Netflix documentary and a Lifetime movie.

title: Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Steve and Nancy Carell celebrated her upcoming 60th birthday at Tresca restaurant in Boston's North End.
Nancy Carell, a Boston College graduate, was born in Cohasset and owns a general store in Marshfield.
The Carells met at an improv class in Chicago and married in 1995, the year Nancy joined 'Saturday Night Live.'

title: Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Saugus residents report excessively high water bills after the town introduced new water meters and a monitoring system.
One resident's water bill unexpectedly surged to $192,000, while others face similar anomalies and have filed abatements.
Town officials are investigating the issue, promising a legal framework for payments as residents face penalties for non-payment.

title: Review & setlist: Bob Dylan was spookily stellar on a smoky night in Boston
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bob Dylan performed at the Leader Bank Pavilion in Boston on July 16, 2026, accompanied by Brittney Spencer and Jimmie Vaughan.
Dylan, at 85, delivered a clear vocal performance, focusing on his recent work while minimally including his classic hits.
The concert featured a 16-song setlist with songs like 'Watchin’ the River Flow,' 'It Ain’t Me, Babe,' and several covers.

title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines' new Airbus A321XLR jets will feature middle seats transformed into tables in Economy Plus.
The table is leather-covered with cup holders and offers extra space for window and aisle seat passengers.
United plans to introduce this feature on 50 A321XLRs for domestic flights later this year and international by 2027.
